process_name: Rare leptonic decay D0 -> e+ e-
implementation_difficulty_reason: D0 -> e+ e- needs a new Delta C = 1 rare leptonic observable with lepton-current normalization, electron-mode acceptance conventions, and long-distance treatment; the existing Delta F = 2 basis does not cover it.

commands: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
rg -n "D0.*e\\+e-|D0.*ee|D\\^0.*e\\+e-|D0.?->.?e|rare.?charm|c.?->.?u.*e" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
evidence: Focused exact C005 search returned no matches in the required implementation/test directories.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:252 and quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:941 are D0 mixing hits, not D0 -> e+ e-.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23 and quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:668 are D0 mixing policy/evaluator hits, not rare leptonic charm.
flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75 is the separate mu -> e gamma LFV dipole check.

rare_charm_model_dependence.topic: Rare charm c -> u l+ l- short-distance and long-distance context

status_history.reason: Initial PKA source extraction and C005 draft deposit.
Writer polish completed for C005 in mixed kaon/charm batch.
All CA checks CHK-1 through CHK-8 passed for C005.
Opus round 1 per-process sign-off; CA-verified PASS retained; see flavor_catalog/signoff/round_001_index.md.

status_history.notes: PDG, Belle, and BABAR D0 -> e+ e- limits verified.

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(\mathcal{B}(D^0\to e^+e^-)\).  This entry
covers the rare neutral-charm leptonic decay \(D^0\to e^+e^-\), a
\(\Delta C=1\), \(c\to u e^+e^-\) flavor-changing neutral-current probe.
Charge-conjugate modes are implicit in the experimental listings.  It is the
electron-mode counterpart to C004, \(D^0\to\mu^+\mu^-\), but has a separate
experimental record.
sections.pdg_prose:
The current PDG Live/API entry \(S032.39\), accessed on 2026-05-16, gives
\[
  \mathcal{B}(D^0\to e^+e^-) < 7.9\times10^{-8}
  \qquad (90\%~{\rm C.L.}).
\]
The sidecar source key is \texttt{PDG2026:D0Ee}, with local snapshot
\texttt{pdg2026\_d0\_ee\_pdgLive.txt}.  The PDG-used input is the Belle 2010
search with \(660\,{\rm fb}^{-1}\) at KEKB.  Belle reports the same
\(7.9\times10^{-8}\) upper limit and no signal evidence
(\texttt{Belle2010:D0Ee};
\texttt{belle2010\_arxiv1003\_2345.txt}).  The independent BaBar 2012 search
used \(468\,{\rm fb}^{-1}\) and set the weaker \(1.7\times10^{-7}\) upper
limit at 90\% CL
(\texttt{BaBar2012:D0EeMuMuEMu};
\texttt{babar2012\_arxiv1206\_5419.txt}).  The dispatch note called this an
LHCb upper limit, but the accessed PDG/API measurement list contains no LHCb
\(D^0\to e^+e^-\) entry
(\texttt{pdg2026\_d0\_ee\_pdgLive.txt}).
sections.relevance:
Anarchic warped models can induce up-sector flavor-changing neutral currents
through nonuniversal KK gauge couplings, flavor-violating \(Z\)-like effects,
Higgs/radion scalar exchange, or lepton-sector extensions
(\texttt{CsakiFalkowskiWeiler2008:CompositeFlavor}).  C005 therefore tests a
pure \(c\to u\) dilepton amplitude rather than the four-quark \(\Delta F=2\)
operator basis used for neutral \(D\)-mixing.  For the current quark-only scan
it is a catalog and roadmap process; a live veto would need both the
\(c\to u\) current and electron-current normalization.
sections.post_2008:
The 2008 RS-flavor baseline predates the Belle and BaBar leptonic \(D^0\)
searches (\texttt{CsakiFalkowskiWeiler2008:CompositeFlavor}).  The main
post-2008 experimental development is Belle's 2010 improvement to the current
PDG limit, followed by BaBar's 2012 cross-check in a separate
\(e^+e^-\to c\bar c\) sample
(\texttt{Belle2010:D0Ee}, \texttt{BaBar2012:D0EeMuMuEMu}).  Unlike C004,
which has newer hadron-collider dimuon searches, the accessed C005 PDG record
still uses the Belle result as the headline input.  Rare-charm theory work
emphasizes that purely leptonic \(D^0\) limits are clean null tests
experimentally but require care when separating short-distance new physics from
long-distance charm dynamics
(\texttt{BurdmanGolowichHewettPakvasa2002:RareCharm}).
sections.validity:
The upper limit itself is experimentally robust.  Its interpretation as an RS
constraint is model dependent because the electron mode is helicity sensitive,
bremsstrahlung and electron identification enter the experimental acceptance,
and the Standard Model rare-charm amplitude is not a precision short-distance
prediction.  A conservative use is an upper bound on additional short-distance
\(c\to u e^+e^-\) amplitudes in a specified operator basis, not a direct
subtraction against a sharply predicted Standard Model rate
(\texttt{BurdmanGolowichHewettPakvasa2002:RareCharm}).
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  The PKA greps and a focused search for
\texttt{D0.*e+e-}, \texttt{D0.*ee}, \texttt{c.?->.?u.*e}, and
\texttt{rare.?charm} found no \(D^0\to e^+e^-\) implementation in
\texttt{quarkConstraints/}, \texttt{qcd/}, \texttt{flavorConstraints/},
\texttt{neutrinos/}, \texttt{yukawa/}, \texttt{warpConfig/},
\texttt{solvers/}, \texttt{scanParams/}, or \texttt{tests/}.  Nearby hits are
not C005: \(D^0\) mixing is configured at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:252} and evaluated at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:941}; the modern policy likewise lists
only \(D^0\) mixing at \texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23}
and \texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:668}.  The
\(\mu\to e\gamma\) dipole check at
\texttt{flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75} is a separate LFV observable.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{HIGH.}  Production use would require a new \(\Delta C=1\) rare
leptonic module with vector, axial-vector, scalar, and pseudoscalar
normalizations, electron-mode acceptance conventions, and a documented
long-distance treatment.  The existing \(\Delta F=2\) SLL/SLR/VLL/VRR/LR
basis is not enough.
sections.key_references:
Process-local keys before bibliography consolidation:
\texttt{PDG2026:D0Ee}, \texttt{Belle2010:D0Ee},
\texttt{BaBar2012:D0EeMuMuEMu},
\texttt{BurdmanGolowichHewettPakvasa2002:RareCharm}, and
\texttt{CsakiFalkowskiWeiler2008:CompositeFlavor}.  Numeric claims above are
tied to these sidecar source keys and local snapshots.

matches.context:
L63: 
L64: Extraction note:
L65:   The current PDG/API headline value for C005 is the Belle 2010 upper limit
L66:   B(D0 -> e+ e-) < 7.9e-8 at 90% CL. No LHCb D0 -> e+ e- measurement appears
L67:   in this PDG API measurement list at the access date above.

L19:   D0 -> e+- mu-+ using 660 fb^-1 of data collected with the Belle detector at
L20:   the KEKB asymmetric-energy e+e- collider. No evidence was found for any of
L21:   these decays. Belle obtained upper limits on the branching fractions:
L22:   B(D0 -> mu+mu-) < 1.4 x 10^-7, B(D0 -> e+e-) < 7.9 x 10^-8, and
L23:   B(D0 -> e+mu-) + B(D0 -> mu+e-) < 2.6 x 10^-7 at 90% confidence level.
L24: 
L25: Extraction note:

L19:   The observed event yields were consistent with expected backgrounds, except
L20:   for a D0 -> mu+mu- upward background fluctuation at the 5% level. Using the
L21:   Feldman-Cousins method, BABAR set 90% confidence-level intervals:
L22:   B(D0 -> e+e-) < 1.7 x 10^-7, B(D0 -> mu+mu-) in [0.6, 8.1] x 10^-7, and
L23:   B(D0 -> e mu) < 3.3 x 10^-7.
L24: 
L25: Extraction note:

priority_rationale_short: D0->ee is helicity-suppressed and far from leading RS reach.
priority_rationale_full:
The D0->e+e- upper limit is experimentally valid, but the channel is
helicity-sensitive and not a clean dominant short-distance charm constraint.
Its RS use would require a dedicated c->u e+e- operator basis and electron
acceptance conventions. D mixing, D-mixing CPV, and muon LFV dominate the
generic anarchic-flavor pressure.
